senses.definition: in a loud, harsh, high-pitched, and piercing way
examples: The brakes squealed and the tires screechingly skidded to a halt.
The train sped away screechingly through the tunnel.

senses.definition: having a harsh, high-pitched sound
examples: The screechy violin made my ears ring.
Her voice sounded screechy when she was upset.

senses.definition: a long, angry speech or piece of writing that expresses strong opinions in a tedious way
examples: The blog post was a political screed about corruption.
He wrote a screed against the new policy that few bothered to read to the end.

senses.definition: past tense of screed; to compose or deliver a screed
examples: He screeded about the injustice for hours.
The op-ed screeded against corporate greed.

senses.definition: someone who writes or delivers a screed
examples: He is the loud screeder in the local blog group.
The author is a notorious screeder on social issues.

senses.definition: plural of screeder; people who write or deliver screeds
examples: The forum attracted several bold screeders.
Screeders in the debate often repeat the same points.

senses.definition: the act of writing or delivering a screed
examples: Her screeding lasted for pages.
The screeding included accusations and complaints.

senses.definition: plural form of screeding; instances of screeds
examples: The blog published several screedings on corruption.
Her screedings often appeared after long nights.

senses.definition: long, often pompous or tedious pieces of writing or speech expressing opinions
examples: His screeds against fashion often miss the point.
The editor dismissed the screed as ranting.

senses.definition: a shallow stream or brook ( Scots/archaic regional usage )
examples: We crossed the screek on stepping stones.
A muddy screek wound through the valley.

senses.definition: a wicker basket or creel used for carrying fish or lobsters
examples: The fisherman carried his catch in a creel.
Hanging the creel by the riverbank, he waited.

senses.definition: a man who makes screeches or shouts; a person who screams; rarely used
examples: The crowd looked for the screeman to start the chant.

senses.definition: a flat surface on which images or text are shown; a barrier that blocks light or people
examples: She watched the movie on a big screen.
The screen kept the wind out.
They installed a screen to divide the room.

senses.definition: to show or display on a screen; to block or protect by screening
examples: The film screens at 7 p.m.
A wall screen screens the courtyard from the street.

senses.definition: a screen iliad protective device; a screen for privacy
examples: A rain screen is installed on the exterior wall.
They used a folding screen in the dressing room.

senses.definition: able to be screened or suitable for screening (e.g., medical tests, content checks)
examples: Only screenable expenses should be claimed.
The content is screenable for age verification.

senses.definition: the period of life when a person typically undergoes screening tests or reaches screen age (informal)
examples: At twenty, he reached screenage for the survey.
The clinic invites people at screenage for vaccination.

senses.definition: a young person who spends a lot of time in front of screens, especially a teenager
examples: Many screenagers prefer texting to talking in person.
Parents worry about screenagers' sleep habits.

senses.definition: plural of screenager; groups of young people who spend much time in front of screens
examples: Screenagers often know more about apps than adults.
Schools notice screenagers may need guidance on digital balance.

senses.definition: skill or artistry in making screen-based media like films or TV shows
examples: He admired the screencraft of that indie film.
The course teaches screencraft from writing to editing.

senses.definition: plural of screencraft; techniques used in screen-based media creation
examples: The guild shares screencrafts for beginners.
Screencrafts vary across genres.

senses.definition: a realm or domain associated with screeches or the screeching sound; metaphorical use
examples: The screendom of the old stage haunted the night.
In the screendom of the chorus, echoes lingered.

senses.definition: protected or examined; selected and approved for a purpose
examples: All applicants were screened before interviews.
The windows were screened to keep insects out.

senses.definition: a person or device that screens; a device or person used to examine or filter
examples: The airport screener checked our bags.
The movie used a digital screener to preview scenes.

senses.definition: plural of screener; people or devices that screen
examples: The screeners at the event were efficient.
We used multiple screeners to verify credentials.

senses.definition: as much as a screen can hold; a large amount that fits on a screen
examples: A screenful of messages flooded the notification pane.
She saved a screenful of photos from the trip.

senses.definition: plural of screenful; multiple portions that fit on a screen
examples: Screens filled with screenfuls of notifications.
We reviewed screenfuls of data.
